disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rhythm feels regular (~100), no dropped-beat runs of irregularity or syncope (against a dangerous arrhythmia)
trigger_question_examples: Is your pulse regular or irregular, and have you fainted?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It feels regular, about 100, and I haven't fainted.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pleuritic chest pain, calf swelling/pain, or immobility; normal SpO2 (against PE); no central crushing chest pain (against ACS)
trigger_question_examples: Any sharp pain on breathing, a swollen painful calf, or recent long travel/surgery?
Any crushing chest pain spreading to your arm/jaw?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No sharp breathing pain, no leg swelling, no recent travel/surgery, and no crushing chest pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began on reading the result; hyperventilating with prior health anxiety; subclinical hypothyroid picture (raised TSH, normal T4)
trigger_question_examples: When did this start relative to reading the result?
Does this feel like previous anxiety?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started when I read the result and it feels like my past health anxiety.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my body shutting down? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen a dangerous arrhythmia, PE and ACS; if the rate is a regular sinus tachycardia with a clear trigger and no red flags, explain subclinical an autoimmune thyroid condition is slow/benign and not an overnight emergency, de-escalate the panic with breathing, and give a cardiac/PE safety-net — without dismissing or over-escalating.
